item.title: LIBRARY
item.description: The scholarly backbone — argument maps of the books and essays the MAP thinks with.

item.title: Hebdige — Subculture
item.description: Author: Dick Hebdige Title: Subculture: The Meaning of Style Year: 1979 Type: Academic book (Methuen) A study of postwar British subcultures — teddy boys, mods, skinheads, punks — that argues style is a form of meaning-making, and that British youth subcultures used Black music as raw material for c...

item.title: George — The Death of Rhythm and Blues
item.description: Author: Nelson George Title: The Death of Rhythm and Blues Year: 1988 Type: Book (Pantheon Books) A history of Black popular music from rhythm and blues through the 1980s that doubles as an argument about institutional destruction.

item.title: Frith — Performing Rites
item.description: Author: Simon Frith Title: Performing Rites: On the Value of Popular Music Year: 1996 Publisher: Harvard University Press Type: Book Frith’s central argument is that value judgments in popular music — what counts as “good,” “authentic,” “serious,” or “real” — describe social processes rather than in...

item.title: Brooks — Liner Notes for the Revolution
(1966) Revolver
item.description: Artist: The Beatles Released: August 5, 1966 Label: Parlophone Producer: George Martin Studio: Abbey Road Studios Genre: Primary: Pop rock, psychedelic pop Secondary: Psychedelic rock, baroque pop RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Revolver is the last Beatles album the band could have performed live, and...

item.title: (1967) Reach Out
item.description: Artist: The Four Tops Released: July 1967 Label: Motown Records Producer: Holland-Dozier-Holland Genre: Primary: Motown sound Secondary: Baroque pop, rhythm & blues, Brill Building RYM · Apple Music · Spotify “Reach Out, I’ll Be There” builds like an emergency.

item.title: (1967) Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
item.description: Artist: The Beatles Released: May 26, 1967 Label: Parlophone Producer: George Martin Studio: Abbey Road Studios Genre: Primary: Psychedelic pop, pop rock Secondary: Psychedelic rock, art rock, baroque pop, sunshine pop, music hall, toytown pop RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Seven hundred hours of stud...

item.title: (1969) Dusty in Memphis
item.description: Artist: Dusty Springfield Released: March 31, 1969 Label: Atlantic Records Producer: Jerry Wexler, Tom Dowd, Arif Mardin Genre: Primary: Pop soul, blue-eyed soul Secondary: Southern soul, smooth soul RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Jerry Wexler brought Dusty Springfield to American Sound Studio in Memp...
